question.text: Under which regulation can listed entities approach SEBI for grievances against proxy advisors?

answer.text: Regulation 24(2) of SEBI (Research Analyst) Regulations, 2014 only
Regulation 4(2)(a) of SEBI (LODR) Regulations, 2015 read with SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations
SEBI examines matters under Regulation 24(2) read with Regulation 23(1) of SEBI (Research Analyst) Regulations, 2014 and the procedural guidelines

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI will examine the matter for non-compliance by proxy advisors with the provisions of the Code of Conduct under Regulation 24(2) read with Regulation 23(1) of SEBI (Research Analyst) Regulations, 2014 and the procedural guidelines. **The Trap:** While LODR Regulation 4(2)(a) is mentioned in the grievance section, it relates to listed entity obligations, not the examination standard. **Cross-Exam Context:** Grievance redressal mechanisms across SEBI regulations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The listed entity approaches SEBI, which then examines proxy advisor compliance with Research Analyst Regulations.
